If you build web apps for a living, one choice is quietly costing you time, money, or sanity.
Read this now if the next project on your desk must scale, ship fast, and survive a furious product pivot.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

— -
Framework wars are not academic. Framework choice can break a launch, double hosting bills, or sink developer morale. That sentence is not dramatic. That sentence is true.
— -
This article is for the developer who must pick a tool and live with that pick for months. It is for the tech lead who wants fewer outages and a faster pipeline. It is for the engineer who cares about craftsmanship and does not want to repeat other people’s costly mistakes.
Expect direct advice. Expect small, real code examples. Expect numbers you can test in an afternoon. Expect a few uncomfortable truths.
— -
Truth 1 Performance is context, not a contest
People quote benchmarks like scripture. Benchmarks are useful only when the scenario matches real life.
The problem
Microbenchmarks show raw render speed. Real apps ship many features, third-party libs, and…
